summary.text: The dominant pattern today is the convergence of attention on agentic workflows and skill libraries, driven by the Claude Fable 5 launch on Hacker News (2,275 votes) and sustained GitHub growth for google/skills (+432 stars/day) and luongnv89/claude-howto (+392 stars/day). AI Agent Frameworks lead category momentum with an average daily delta of 424, while Coding Agents average 655 stars/day, indicating developer hunger for reusable, composable agent components. Meanwhile, Local LLM / Inference sees a sudden breakout for NextChat and DeepSpeed, but with low absolute deltas (24 and 11 stars/day), suggesting niche interest rather than a broad shift. The absence of new Product Hunt launches in AI today is notable, possibly because all attention is absorbed by the Claude Fable 5 discussion. If this pattern holds, expect more curated skill marketplaces and agent orchestration tools to emerge, as developers move beyond single-model chat to multi-agent systems.

projects.name: skills
projects.description: Agent Skills for Google products and technologies

llmSummary.oneLiner: A curated collection of reusable agent skills for Google Cloud and Google products, installable via a single CLI command.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers building AI agents need a standardized way to integrate Google Cloud services (Gemini, BigQuery, Cloud Run, etc.) without writing boilerplate code. This repo provides pre-built, composable skills that can be added to any agent framework.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 12,994 stars and a 24-hour delta of +432 stars, this repo is experiencing sustained high attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike. The consistent velocity suggests broad developer interest in agentic workflows on Google Cloud.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits (within 7 days) and active issue tracking show ongoing development, but no major release or external event is evident from the metadata.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Google's push into agentic AI and the simplicity of `npx skills add google/skills` lowers the barrier to integrating Google Cloud capabilities into agents. The repo covers essential services (Gemini, BigQuery, Cloud Run, Kubernetes) and best-practice recipes (onboarding, auth, networking), making it a one-stop shop for Google Cloud agent development.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you're building agents on Google Cloud, this repo is the fastest way to add production-ready skills. The modular design lets you pick only what you need, and the CLI install is frictionless.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward skill-marketplace models for AI agents, where reusable, cloud-specific capabilities are distributed as packages. Developers should expect more such curated skill sets from major cloud providers, reducing the need to reinvent integrations.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI/ML engineers, cloud developers, and agent builders who use Google Cloud and want to quickly add Gemini, BigQuery, Cloud Run, or other Google services to their agents.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Skills are under active development and may change. The repo is Google-maintained but not officially supported as a product. Some skills may be thin wrappers around APIs, and long-term maintenance is uncertain.

projects.name: claude-howto
projects.description: A visual, example-driven guide to Claude Code — from basic concepts to advanced agents, with copy-paste templates that bring immediate value.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A structured, visual, example-driven guide to mastering Claude Code from beginner to advanced agent orchestration, with copy-paste templates and a progressive learning path.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Claude Code's official docs describe features but don't show how to combine them into real workflows; there's no clear learning path, and examples are too basic. This guide fills that gap with production-ready templates, Mermaid diagrams, and a guided roadmap.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 36,306 stars, 4,398 forks, and a 24-hour delta of +392 stars, this repo is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio is low (1), indicating steady organic interest rather than a sudden spike. It's actively maintained (latest release v2.1.160, June 2026) and has recent commits within 7 days, signaling ongoing relevance.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated with a new version badge (v2.1.160) and the repo has a recent release within 30 days, but no external buzz or sudden acceleration is detected.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It addresses a clear pain point for Claude Code users: the gap between basic usage and advanced workflow orchestration. The combination of visual tutorials, copy-paste templates, and a structured learning path makes it immediately valuable. The MIT license and community-driven contributions further amplify its appeal.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use Claude Code, this guide is the fastest way to unlock advanced features like hooks, subagents, MCP, and plugins. The templates are production-ready and can be copied into your project in minutes.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a growing demand for practical, example-driven resources around AI coding agents. Developers want more than API docs — they need battle-tested patterns and workflows. The success of this repo suggests that the next wave of AI tooling adoption will be driven by community-curated guides and templates.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Claude Code users of all levels — from beginners who just installed the CLI to advanced users looking to orchestrate multi-agent workflows. Also relevant for teams adopting Claude Code for code review, CI/CD, documentation, and security audits.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The guide is tied to Claude Code's evolution; if Anthropic changes the API or feature set significantly, some templates may break. Also, the repo's value depends on continued maintenance and community contributions.

projects.name: NextChat
projects.description: ✨ Light and Fast AI Assistant. Support: Web | iOS | MacOS | Android |  Linux | Windows

llmSummary.oneLiner: NextChat is a lightweight, cross-platform AI chat client that supports multiple LLMs (Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, DeepSeek) with one-click deployment and a privacy-first local-storage design.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It provides a unified, fast, and privacy-respecting interface for interacting with various AI models, eliminating the need to switch between different chat UIs or expose API keys to third-party services.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 88,131 stars and a 24h star delta of 22, the project shows sustained high interest. The acceleration ratio of 2.9 (high) indicates a sudden breakout in attention, likely driven by recent feature additions like MCP support, real-time chat, and the iOS app release. The 7-day average star delta of 7.8 confirms consistent growth, but the acceleration signals a recent spike.
llmSummary.whatChanged: Recent commits and README updates highlight the addition of MCP (Model Context Protocol) support, real-time chat, and the launch of the iOS app. These features expand the project's utility and appeal, likely triggering the breakout.
llmSummary.whyTrending: NextChat combines ease of deployment (one-click Vercel), broad model support, and a polished cross-platform desktop app, making it a go-to tool for developers and power users who want a self-hosted, customizable AI assistant.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This project demonstrates the demand for lightweight, multi-model chat UIs that prioritize privacy and local storage. Developers can learn from its architecture (Next.js + Tauri) and plugin system to build similar tools.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rapid adoption of NextChat signals a shift toward unified, self-hosted AI interfaces that reduce dependency on single providers. The MCP support hints at a future where AI tools interoperate via standardized protocols, opening opportunities for plugin ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers, AI enthusiasts, and power users who want a fast, private, and customizable chat interface for multiple LLMs, including those running local models.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party API keys and services; users must manage their own keys. The rapid pace of features may introduce instability. No recent release tag could indicate a focus on bleeding-edge commits over stable releases.

projects.name: DeepSpeed
projects.description: DeepSpeed is a deep learning optimization library that makes distributed training and inference easy, efficient, and effective.

llmSummary.oneLiner: DeepSpeed is a deep learning optimization library for efficient distributed training and inference of large models.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Training and inference of large deep learning models (billions to trillions of parameters) is challenging due to memory and compute constraints. DeepSpeed provides system innovations like ZeRO, 3D parallelism, and offloading to make large-scale training and inference efficient, easy, and scalable.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo shows a sudden breakout with a 4.1x acceleration ratio and 11 stars in the last 24 hours, totaling 42,494 stars. This indicates a recent surge in attention, likely driven by new features or community events.
llmSummary.whatChanged: Recent activity includes a new release within 30 days, a blog post on Muon Optimizer support (May 2026), and a tutorial at ASPLOS 2026. The project also received an Honorable Mention for Best Paper at ASPLOS 2026 for SuperOffload. These developments likely contributed to the spike.
llmSummary.whyTrending: DeepSpeed is a foundational tool for training large models, and its continuous innovation (e.g., Muon Optimizer, SDMA for AMD GPUs, SuperOffload) keeps it relevant. The ASPLOS recognition and active community engagement (office hours, meetups) drive sustained interest.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: DeepSpeed is essential for anyone training or deploying large models on GPUs. Its support for multiple hardware backends (NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, Huawei) and integration with major frameworks (Hugging Face, Lightning) makes it a versatile choice.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rapid adoption of new optimizers (Muon) and hardware-specific features (SDMA) signals a trend toward more specialized, efficient training techniques. Developers should monitor DeepSpeed's roadmap for future optimizations.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Deep learning engineers and researchers working on large-scale model training and inference, especially those using PyTorch and needing memory-efficient parallelism.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has a high number of open issues (1,297), which may indicate maintenance challenges or complexity. Users should ensure compatibility with their specific hardware and software stack.

projects.name: netdata
projects.description: The fastest path to AI-powered full stack observability, even for lean teams.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Netdata is an open-source, real-time infrastructure monitoring platform with per-second metrics, zero-configuration setup, and built-in ML-powered anomaly detection.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It eliminates the complexity and cost of traditional monitoring by providing instant, high-resolution visibility into every metric across servers, containers, and applications, without requiring central data storage or complex configuration.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 79,102 stars and a sudden breakout acceleration of 3 (high), Netdata is experiencing a sharp uptick in attention. The 24-hour star delta of 14 and 7-day delta of 63 indicate growing momentum, likely driven by its AI-powered observability features and the broader industry shift toward real-time, ML-enhanced monitoring.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits and ongoing community engagement suggest continuous improvement, but no specific release or external mention is evident from the data.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Netdata's combination of per-second granularity, zero-config deployment, and edge-based ML anomaly detection addresses a critical pain point for DevOps and SRE teams seeking lightweight, scalable observability without vendor lock-in or high costs.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Netdata offers a drop-in monitoring solution that works out of the box, with automatic discovery of services and metrics. Developers can integrate it into existing stacks via Docker, Kubernetes, or direct install, and leverage its API for custom dashboards or alerting.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward real-time, AI-native observability tools that run at the edge. For AI developers, Netdata's ML models trained per metric at the edge represent a practical pattern for anomaly detection without centralizing data, reducing latency and cost.
llmSummary.targetUsers: DevOps engineers, SREs, system administrators, and platform teams managing Linux, macOS, FreeBSD, or Windows servers, containers, or Kubernetes clusters who need instant, detailed visibility into infrastructure health.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is large and complex; new users may face a learning curve despite zero-config claims. The GPL v3+ license may restrict commercial embedding. No recent release tag could indicate slower release cadence or focus on nightly builds.

projects.name: Front-End-Checklist
projects.description: 🗂 The essential checklist for modern web development, for humans and AI agents

llmSummary.oneLiner: A comprehensive front-end quality checklist with 385 rules, now exposed via MCP for AI agents.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a structured, actionable checklist of front-end best practices (HTML, CSS, JS, accessibility, performance, SEO, security, etc.) that both humans and AI agents can use to audit and improve web projects.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 72,871 stars and a sudden breakout acceleration of 2.3 (high), the project is experiencing a sharp uptick in attention. The 24h star delta of 18 and 7d delta of 82 indicate sustained interest, while the acceleration suggests a recent catalyst. The MCP server integration makes it immediately useful for AI agents, aligning with the current surge in agentic workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The recent addition of an MCP server (mcp.frontendchecklist.io) and explicit AI agent support in the README, combined with a release within the last 30 days, likely drove the sudden breakout. The project also added skills for reusable audit workflows.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It bridges the gap between human-readable checklists and AI agent tooling, making it a practical resource for developers building or reviewing front-end code with AI assistance. The large rule corpus (385 rules) and MCP compatibility make it a go-to reference for automated code review.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Integrate this checklist into your CI/CD or AI agent workflows to automate front-end quality checks. The MCP server allows agents to audit code, URLs, and fetch rules programmatically.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward structured, machine-readable best practices that AI agents can consume directly. Developers should expect more projects to expose rule sets via MCP or similar protocols, enabling automated, context-aware code review.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Front-end developers, web developers, QA engineers, and AI/agent developers building automated code review or audit tools.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The checklist is opinionated and may not cover all edge cases. Over-reliance on automated checks could miss context-specific issues. The project's sudden breakout may attract noise or unsustainable hype.

projects.name: ai-engineering-from-scratch
projects.description: Learn it. Build it. Ship it for others.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to autonomous swarms, with every lesson producing a reusable artifact.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Bridges the gap between scattered AI tutorials and deep, practical understanding. Most learners use AI tools but can't explain how they work; this curriculum ensures you build every algorithm from scratch before using frameworks.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 30,905 stars, a 7-day delta of 9,805 (avg 1,553/day), and a 24h delta of 486, this repo is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.9 indicates strong organic momentum without a sudden spike, suggesting broad, steady adoption by the developer community.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README mentions 150,639 readers and 241,669 page views in the last 30 days, indicating consistent community engagement.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It fills a clear need: a structured, end-to-end curriculum that goes from math to production, with reusable outputs (prompts, skills, agents, MCP servers). The 'build it, use it, ship it' loop resonates with developers who want deep understanding, not just API usage.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a comprehensive resource for anyone serious about AI engineering. It's free, MIT-licensed, and covers modern topics like MCP, agent loops, and swarms. Developers can use it to level up from practitioner to builder.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Signals a shift toward 'full-stack AI' education — developers want to understand internals (attention, backprop) alongside production deployment (agents, MCP). Projects that combine theory with shippable artifacts are gaining traction.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and engineers who want to deeply understand AI/ML from first principles, build their own agents and models, and create production-ready artifacts. Suitable for self-learners, bootcamp grads, and experienced devs pivoting into AI.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The curriculum is massive (~320 hours) and may overwhelm beginners. Some lessons may be incomplete or in progress (61 open issues). The project's long-term maintenance depends on the creator's continued effort.

projects.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
projects.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go with prefix-cache stability and plugin-driven architecture.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a configurable, multi-model AI coding agent that runs as a single static binary, optimized for DeepSeek's prefix caching to reduce token costs during long sessions.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 20,646 stars, a 24-hour delta of +707, and a 7-day average delta of 544.1, this project is experiencing sustained high growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.5 indicates strong, steady momentum without sudden spikes, suggesting broad developer adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent 1.0 rewrite in Go and active development (commits within 7 days, recent release) likely contribute to continued interest.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines the popularity of DeepSeek models with a polished, config-driven terminal agent that supports plugins (MCP), multi-model setups, and sandboxed execution, appealing to developers seeking efficient AI-assisted coding workflows.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: A practical, production-ready agent for DeepSeek users that emphasizes low token costs and extensibility via plugins and config files.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Signals a shift toward lightweight, binary-distributed AI agents that leverage model-specific optimizations (like prefix caching) and community-driven plugin ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using DeepSeek models who want a terminal-based AI coding assistant with customizable tools, permissions, and multi-model support.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-caching behavior; if DeepSeek changes its caching model, performance may degrade. Also, 303 open issues suggest active but potentially buggy development.

projects.name: agent-skills
projects.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A curated pack of 23 production-grade engineering skills and 7 slash commands that encode senior engineer workflows for AI coding agents across the full development lifecycle.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents often produce inconsistent, low-quality output because they lack the structured workflows, quality gates, and best practices that senior engineers follow. Agent Skills packages these into reusable Markdown skills that agents can invoke via slash commands or auto-detect, ensuring every phase from spec to ship follows proven patterns.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 49,949 stars and a 595-star spike in the last 24 hours (acceleration ratio >2.0), this repo is experiencing a sudden breakout. The 7-day delta of 2,147 stars and a 3-day average of 292 stars/day confirm intense, accelerating attention. This is not gradual growth — it's a viral moment driven by the rising adoption of agentic coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini CLI.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent release (within 30 days) and active commits (within 7 days) suggest ongoing refinement. The breakout likely correlates with the broader surge in AI coding agent usage and the release of compatible tools (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor) that make these skills immediately actionable.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Agent Skills fills a critical gap: it provides a standardized, high-quality skill library that works across multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, Copilot, etc.). As developers increasingly rely on AI agents for production code, they need guardrails and workflows — this repo delivers that in a simple, portable format.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a must-try for any developer using AI coding agents in production. The skills are practical, well-documented, and cover the entire lifecycle. The `/build auto` command alone could significantly reduce manual oversight while maintaining quality.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rapid adoption signals a shift: developers are no longer satisfied with raw LLM output — they want structured, engineer-approved workflows. This repo represents a new category of 'agent middleware' that could become as essential as linters or CI/CD pipelines.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, Copilot) who want to enforce consistent, production-grade engineering practices without manual oversight.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The skills are opinionated and may not fit every team's workflow. Over-reliance on auto-commands could reduce developer understanding of the underlying practices. The project is new and rapidly evolving — breaking changes possible.

projects.name: career-ops
projects.description: AI-powered job search system built on Claude Code. 14 skill modes, Go dashboard, PDF generation, batch processing.

llmSummary.oneLiner: An open-source AI-powered job search command center that turns any AI coding CLI into a pipeline for evaluating, tailoring, and tracking job applications.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Job seekers waste time manually tracking applications and tailoring resumes. Career-Ops automates the entire pipeline: evaluates job offers with a structured scoring system, generates ATS-optimized PDFs, scans job portals, and tracks everything in a single source of truth.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo is exploding: 1,556 stars in the last 24 hours, 3,875 in the last 7 days, with a 24-hour delta of 893 and a 3-day average of 600.3. The acceleration ratio of 2.6 indicates a sudden breakout, not just steady growth. With 52,271 total stars and 10,485 forks, it's already a massive project that's still accelerating.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The project recently released v1.9.0 (within 30 days) and has active commits within the last 7 days. The README mentions being featured in WIRED and Business Insider, which likely drove a surge of attention. The Product Hunt badge and Discord community also suggest recent promotional activity.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It directly addresses a universal pain point—job searching—with a practical, AI-driven solution that promises to save hours. The combination of a compelling personal story ('I used this to land my dream role'), concrete results (740+ evaluations, 100+ CVs), and broad AI CLI support (Claude, Gemini, Codex, Qwen) makes it highly shareable.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a well-engineered, opinionated tool that demonstrates how to build agentic workflows with AI CLIs. Developers can learn from its modular design (modes, archetypes, scoring) and its human-in-the-loop philosophy. It's also a great example of a project that went viral by solving a real problem.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a shift toward AI-assisted career management, where candidates use AI to filter companies rather than the other way around. For AI developers, it shows the power of combining multiple AI models (Claude, Gemini, etc.) with automation tools (Playwright, Go dashboard) to create a cohesive, practical system.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Job seekers in tech, especially those targeting AI/ML roles; developers comfortable with CLI tools; anyone tired of manual job applications and willing to invest in setup for long-term efficiency.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The system requires trust in AI evaluations and may produce poor results initially until it learns the user's context. It's not a 'spray-and-pray' tool—users must review before applying. The project is MIT-licensed but has a trademark policy, which could limit commercial use.

modelDemoSignals.title: When to Align, When to Predict: A Phase Diagram for Multimodal Learning
modelDemoSignals.description: Cross-modal alignment (CA) and cross-modal prediction (CP) are the dominant paradigms for multimodal representation learning, yet there is no systematic understanding of when each succeeds, when each fails, and when cross-modal training helps at all -- a gap that leaves practitioners, especially in scientific domains like biomedicine or astrophysics, with heterogeneous instruments and multiple levels of organization and measurement, unable to diagnose why standard methods underperform the best s
modelDemoSignals.summary: Cross-modal alignment (CA) and cross-modal prediction (CP) are the dominant paradigms for multimodal representation learning, yet there is no systematic understanding of when each succeeds, when each fails, and when cross-modal training helps at all -- a gap that leaves practitioners, especially in scientific domains like biomedicine or astrophysics, with heterogeneous instruments and multiple levels of organization and measurement, unable to diagnose why standard methods underperform the best single modality. We develop a unified linear framework that addresses both questions. Under a spiked signal-plus-noise model with structured cross-modal nuisance correlation, we derive separation ratios for both objectives that expose complementary failure modes: alignment whitens each modality and fails when nuisance is strongly correlated across views; prediction encodes whatever is cross-predictable through a one-sided whitening, with recovery governed by source-modality quality. The resulting phase diagram partitions multimodal problems into four regimes: Both, CA only, CP only, and Neither. We present a data-driven procedure to locate real-world datasets in this diagram using a small labeled subsample, identifying the preferred objective and prediction direction before any cross-modal training. Experiments on synthetic data, stereo-vision benchmarks, image-caption pairs, and real astrophysical data validate the predictions in the nonlinear regime, including the Neither regime where cross-modal training is actively harmful. Our framework lets practitioners diagnose their multimodal problem and choose the right objective before committing to training. Code to reproduce the results is available at https://github.com/IlayMalinyak/mm_align_vs_pred.
modelDemoSignals.author: Ilay Kamai, Hugues Van Assel, Aviv Regev, Hagai B. Perets, Randall Balestriero
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: alignment, benchmark

modelDemoSignals.title: A Unifying Lens on Supervised Fine-Tuning Through Target Distribution Design
modelDemoSignals.description: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) typically maximizes the likelihood of every token in a demonstrated trajectory. However, an observed token can be non-unique, noisy, or misaligned with the model prior. Strictly fitting toward this one-hot target may be suboptimal, especially when the pretrained model encodes a rich knowledge prior. In this work, we reinterpret SFT as target distribution design: instead of studying only the loss objective, we analyze the token-level target that the loss drives the mo
modelDemoSignals.summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) typically maximizes the likelihood of every token in a demonstrated trajectory. However, an observed token can be non-unique, noisy, or misaligned with the model prior. Strictly fitting toward this one-hot target may be suboptimal, especially when the pretrained model encodes a rich knowledge prior. In this work, we reinterpret SFT as target distribution design: instead of studying only the loss objective, we analyze the token-level target that the loss drives the model to match. We introduce the Q-target framework, which decomposes SFT supervision into two explicit choices: (1) how strongly to rely on the observed token, and (2) how to allocate the remaining probability mass over alternatives. This perspective unifies many existing SFT variants as implicit choices of the target distribution Q. Building on this view, we propose Target-SFT which constructs the training objective directly from the desired target distribution. This method consistently outperforms across the ten reasoning dataset-model settings evaluated, showing the effectiveness of this target-based approach. Overall, our formulation reveals a more fundamental design principle for SFT training and opens a broader search space for SFT objectives.
modelDemoSignals.author: Tong Xie, Yuanhao Ban, Yunqi Hong, Sohyun An, Yihang Chen, Cho-Jui Hsieh

modelDemoSignals.title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents
modelDemoSignals.description: In this paper, we propose EEVEE, the first multi-dataset test-time prompt learning framework for LLM agents, enabling test-time prompt learning under real-world task streams. Existing methods are largely designed for single-dataset settings, while real-world applications require models to handle heterogeneous input streams drawn from multiple datasets, domains, and task distributions, limiting their practical applicability. To mitigate cross-dataset interference, EEVEE introduces a router that p
modelDemoSignals.summary: In this paper, we propose EEVEE, the first multi-dataset test-time prompt learning framework for LLM agents, enabling test-time prompt learning under real-world task streams. Existing methods are largely designed for single-dataset settings, while real-world applications require models to handle heterogeneous input streams drawn from multiple datasets, domains, and task distributions, limiting their practical applicability. To mitigate cross-dataset interference, EEVEE introduces a router that partitions incoming inputs into task clusters and assigns them to suitable prompt configurations. This design is optimized via a router-prompt co-evolution strategy, which employs interleaved router and prompt learning phases to address their mutual dependency. Experiments across multiple datasets demonstrate that the framework improves robustness under heterogeneous data streams while maintaining single-benchmark learning capability and efficiency. Specifically, EEVEE improves average multi-benchmark scores by 10.38 and 24.32 points over Qwen3-4B-Instruct and DeepSeek-V3.2, surpassing SOTA methods GEPA and ACE by up to 37.2% and 48.2%.
modelDemoSignals.author: Weixian Xu, Shilong Liu, Mengdi Wang
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, rag, benchmark

modelDemoSignals.title: Data Journalist Agent: Transforming Data into Verifiable Multimodal Stories
modelDemoSignals.description: Data tells stories that shape society; the data journalist's job is to turn raw information into stories non-experts can trust. A high-quality news feature takes a newsroom team weeks: hunting for context, running statistics, choosing an angle, and designing visuals. Recent agents handle individual steps well: data-science agents close the analysis loop, while design agents synthesize beautiful websites. But can an agent serve as a data journalist end to end? We introduce Data Journalist Agent (
modelDemoSignals.summary: Data tells stories that shape society; the data journalist's job is to turn raw information into stories non-experts can trust. A high-quality news feature takes a newsroom team weeks: hunting for context, running statistics, choosing an angle, and designing visuals. Recent agents handle individual steps well: data-science agents close the analysis loop, while design agents synthesize beautiful websites. But can an agent serve as a data journalist end to end? We introduce Data Journalist Agent (Data2Story), a multi-agent framework that orchestrates specialized roles into a single virtual newsroom. Data2Story contributes two innovations. (i) Claims are evidence-grounded: an Inspector links every number, angle, and asset back to data, code, or an external reference. (ii) Articles are multimodally generative: rather than defaulting to plain text and static charts, Data2Story reasons about what readers will want to see, then deploys multimodal tools, such as interactive maps for geography and audio for music. We evaluate Data2Story on 18 articles, each paired with the originally published expert piece, along four axes: (a) human-agent angle coverage; (b) rubric evaluation with 53 participants across five dimensions; (c) computer-use agents as judges, a cost-saving proxy for how readers navigate interactive articles; and (d) verifiability, where a coding verifier re-executes statements against the data and checks claims against references. Data2Story produces competitive, evidence-traceable multimedia stories, with particular strength in transparency and auditability. Human articles retain an edge in editorial angle, creative design, and presentation. We position Data2Story as a collaborator for journalists, enabling more evidence-based, transparent, and verifiable reporting. Code and demos are available at https://data2story.github.io.
modelDemoSignals.author: Kevin Qinghong Lin, Batu EI, Yuhong Shi, Pan Lu, Philip Torr, James Zou

modelDemoSignals.title: The Role of Feedback Alignment in Self-Distillation
modelDemoSignals.description: Conditioning a language model on additional context, such as feedback on a previous attempt, typically improves its response. Self-distillation trains the model to retain this improvement when the context is not present. The method works by matching the model's output distribution under two settings: a student that sees only the question, and a self-teacher that also sees the context. What the model learns therefore depends on what context the self-teacher receives, yet the design of this contex
modelDemoSignals.summary: Conditioning a language model on additional context, such as feedback on a previous attempt, typically improves its response. Self-distillation trains the model to retain this improvement when the context is not present. The method works by matching the model's output distribution under two settings: a student that sees only the question, and a self-teacher that also sees the context. What the model learns therefore depends on what context the self-teacher receives, yet the design of this context remains largely unexplored. We study context design for self-distillation by training a solver on feedback from a frozen critic. We compare three conditions: (i) a binary reward (GRPO), (ii) the reference solution, and (iii) a step-by-step critique aligned to the solver's reasoning trace. Step-aligned critique yields the largest gains, outperforming GRPO by 16.11 points and reference-solution-conditioned self-distillation by 5.27 points (Avg@12). Per-token advantage analysis reveals why: step-aligned feedback targets only the tokens where reasoning fails, leaving correct behavior intact. Conditioning on the reference solution, by contrast, pressures the model to change its behavior at every token (even correct steps) because an alternative derivation inevitably differs in phrasing and approach. This suggests that structural alignment between feedback and the solver's reasoning is a key driver of self-distillation effectiveness.
modelDemoSignals.author: Semih Kara, Oğuzhan Ersoy

aihotHighlights.title: 百度百舸联合复旦提出LU-KV框架，被ICML 2026录用
aihotHighlights.summary: 百度百舸团队与复旦大学合作提出Long-horizon Utility KV（LU-KV）框架，将头级KV Cache预算分配建模为面向长程边际效用的全局组合优化问题。LU-KV通过离线画像估计注意力头边际贡献曲线，结合凸包松弛与基于边际效用的贪心求解器，在较低开销下得到接近最优的预算配置，可适配SnapKV、KeyDiff等多类压缩方法。在LongBench和RULER基准上，80%压缩比下性能损失小，降低显存占用和推理延迟。相关论文被ICML 2026录用。

aihotHighlights.title: 谷歌 DeepMind 经济学家伊马斯：尚未发现 AI 造成岗位流失的证据，跟风裁员恐适得其反
aihotHighlights.summary: 谷歌 DeepMind AGI 经济学负责人亚历克斯·伊马斯表示，目前没有看到白领岗位因 AI 大规模消失的证据。他强调，若企业因"不裁员就等于 AI 转型慢"的叙事而跟风裁员，可能适得其反。伊马斯认为，AI 更多是接手部分任务、提升生产力，让员工专注机器无法完成的工作，岗位冲击尚未真正出现。

aihotHighlights.title: 摩尔线程开源 MusaCoder 代码大模型，9B/27B 参数基于国产 GPU 全链路训练
aihotHighlights.summary: 摩尔线程发布并开源 MusaCoder 代码大模型，含 9B 和 27B 两个参数规模，是业内首个基于国产 GPU 算力底座完成全链路训练与验证的开源模型。后训练流程在基于 MTT S5000 的夸娥智算集群上完成，支持从 PyTorch 标准算子自动生成高性能 CUDA/MUSA 原生 Kernel 代码。在 KernelBench 评测中，MusaCoder-27B-RL 以 Overall Pass@8 93.2%、Avg.@8 88.60% 超越 Claude Opus、DeepSeek-V4 Pro、GLM-5.1、Kimi K2.6 等主流 SOTA 代码模型。

aihotHighlights.summary: 宝玉分享了5点心得：1. 加入设计系统（如 Adobe Spectrum 2）可避免 AI 味，设为默认后可专注布局与交互。2. 先搭建少量功能，再通过左侧聊天框逐步调整。3. 用 Markup 框选局部评论，Edit 可手动调整元素树。4. 注意上下文管理，新任务创建新会话。5. 通过 Tweaks 面板调整主题、布局、加载状态，也可添加导航快速切换界面。

aihotHighlights.title: 火山方舟版权商业化平台上线，周星驰比高集团三大电影IP首批入驻
aihotHighlights.summary: 火山引擎今日上线火山方舟版权商业化平台，推出行业首个覆盖"授权-保护-审核-分发-变现"全链路的版权合作机制。平台搭载视频生成模型Seedance 2.0及版权治理体系，已获周星驰旗下比高集团《喜剧之王》《食神》《长江七号》三部影片在AI视频创作场景下的版权使用权，并基于Seedance 2.0打造经典桥段AI创作模板。模板已在火山方舟体验中心、火山引擎Kickart上线，同步开放给LibTV、筷子科技丽帧引擎等工具合作伙伴。平台面向UGC和商业广告场景提供分润制、项目制等变现路径，未来还将提供版权管理后台，实现授权可见、使用可查、收入透明。

aihotHighlights.title: Magnetar用数百AI智能体替代分析师
aihotHighlights.summary: Bloomberg：Magnetar Capital，这家 180 亿美元的对冲基金公司，将在其最新产品中避免使用人类分析师，转而依靠数百个 AI 智能体进行股票研究。 这家 180 亿美元的对冲基金公司希望 AI 搜索投资想法、研究公司、推荐头寸并预测趋势，而人类仍负责批准交易。

aihotHighlights.title: 欧盟发布临时措施，要求 Meta 向第三方 AI 助手免费开放 WhatsApp
aihotHighlights.summary: 欧盟委员会6月9日宣布临时措施，责令Meta在反垄断调查结束前免费向第三方AI助手开放WhatsApp访问权限。Meta于2025年10月15日禁止第三方AI助手调用WhatsApp for Business API，意图让自家Meta AI独占市场；今年3月4日虽改为付费使用，但欧盟委员会认为这实质上延续了禁令，可能严重损害通用AI助手市场竞争，尤其危及小企业和新进入者。

aihotHighlights.title: Text-To-Lottie：Agent Skill + 本地预览 Harness，让 Agent 生成 Lottie 动画并实时验收
aihotHighlights.summary: 开源项目 Text-To-Lottie 提供一套 Agent Skill 和本地预览工具，让 Codex/Claude Code/Cursor 等 Agent 生成标准 Bodymovin JSON（public/lottie.json），通过 Skottie 渲染引擎在浏览器中实时验收。安装命令：`npx skills add diffusionstudio/lottie`。技术特点：输出标准 Lottie JSON，使用 Skottie 渲染（非 lottie-web）；基于 Vite 热重载实现自动刷新闭环；支持通过 Skottie Slots + controls.json 调整颜色、尺寸；Agent 可用 URL 参数 `？frame=60&paused=1` 精确定位帧截图。Prompt 指南包括：给具体素材、使用动效术语、pan/zoom/hold 模拟镜头、显式声明 Slots、锁定时间规格。适合单场景短时长 Motion Graphics、SVG→Lottie、数据可视化、透明背景矢量动效；不适合多镜头剪辑、复杂角色绑定、粒子、3D 或需 lottie-web 全特性的场景。

aihotHighlights.title: IBM CEO：AI不一定导致员工减少
aihotHighlights.summary: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna表示AI不会必然导致员工数量减少。他透露IBM已在量子计算（一种更快形式的AI）上投资100亿美元，并指出联邦政府承诺投入10亿美元在纽约Albany建设芯片制造设施，体现了公私部门间的紧密合作。

aihotHighlights.title: 在 AgentsView 中为 Claude Fable 5 设置自定义价格
aihotHighlights.summary: Wes McKinney 开发的 AgentsView 是一个用于追踪本地编码智能体 token 使用情况的工具。由于近日发布的 Claude Fable 5 尚未被收录进 AgentsView 的定价数据库，作者利用 Fable 逆向工程，找到了为该模型设置自定义价格的方法，并展示了 Fable 5 当天在不同本地项目中的使用量树状图。

crossSourceHighlights.title: Claude Fable 5 和 Claude Mythos 5

items.title: Claude Fable 5 和 Claude Mythos 5
items.summary: Anthropic 今日推出 Claude Fable 5（通用安全版）和 Claude Mythos 5（受限安全版）。Fable 5 在软件工程、知识工作、视觉、科研等几乎所有测试基准上达到 SOTA，Stripe 称其将数月工程压缩至数天，FrontierCode 评分居前沿模型之首，可仅凭截图重建网页应用源码。Mythos 5 在药物设计中实现约 10 倍加速，其分子生物学假说盲测获科学家偏好的概率约 80%。两模型售价均为 $10/百万输入 tokens、$50/百万输出 tokens，较 Claude Mythos Preview 降价过半。Fable 5 在部分敏感主题上回退至 Claude Opus 4.8，安全触发率低于 5% 的会话。Mythos 5 通过 Project Glasswing 向网络安全防御者开放。

items.title: 百度百舸联合复旦提出LU-KV框架，被ICML 2026录用
items.description: 百度百舸团队与复旦大学合作提出Long-horizon Utility KV（LU-KV）框架，将头级KV Cache预算分配建模为面向长程边际效用的全局组合优化问题。LU-KV通过离线画像估计注意力头边际贡献曲线，结合凸包松弛与基于边际效用的贪心求解器，在较低开销下得到接近最优的预算配置，可适配SnapKV、KeyDiff等多类压缩方法。在LongBench和RULER基准上，80%压缩比下性能损失小，降低显存占用和推理延迟。相关论文被ICML 2026录用。

items.title: 华为云发布全球首个端到端具身AI平台CloudRobo
items.description: 华为云推出全球首个端到端具身AI开发平台CloudRobo，覆盖从数据、模型到部署、集成的全生命周期，基于PB级可信数据底座。在INSPIRE2026上，国家地方共建人形机器人创新中心、Yijiahe Technology、上海交通大学展示了其核心能力：数据与模型双评估系统、主动力控模型快速组装、机器人小时级上云、模型分钟级部署。

items.title: 谷歌 DeepMind 经济学家伊马斯：尚未发现 AI 造成岗位流失的证据，跟风裁员恐适得其反
items.description: 谷歌 DeepMind AGI 经济学负责人亚历克斯·伊马斯表示，目前没有看到白领岗位因 AI 大规模消失的证据。他强调，若企业因"不裁员就等于 AI 转型慢"的叙事而跟风裁员，可能适得其反。伊马斯认为，AI 更多是接手部分任务、提升生产力，让员工专注机器无法完成的工作，岗位冲击尚未真正出现。

items.title: 摩尔线程开源 MusaCoder 代码大模型，9B/27B 参数基于国产 GPU 全链路训练
items.description: 摩尔线程发布并开源 MusaCoder 代码大模型，含 9B 和 27B 两个参数规模，是业内首个基于国产 GPU 算力底座完成全链路训练与验证的开源模型。后训练流程在基于 MTT S5000 的夸娥智算集群上完成，支持从 PyTorch 标准算子自动生成高性能 CUDA/MUSA 原生 Kernel 代码。在 KernelBench 评测中，MusaCoder-27B-RL 以 Overall Pass@8 93.2%、Avg.@8 88.60% 超越 Claude Opus、DeepSeek-V4 Pro、GLM-5.1、Kimi K2.6 等主流 SOTA 代码模型。

items.title: 工信部印发《"人工智能+信息通信"创新发展实施意见》
items.description: 工信部发文，要求加快建设400Gbps/800Gbps骨干传输网络，优化东中西部国家枢纽节点间通道；推进城域400Gbps及以上、全光交叉等高速光传输系统应用，构建城域毫秒级低时延入算能力。同时推动5G-A/6G、新一代光网络、"IPv6+"、工业互联网与AI融合发展，攻关空口智能化、网络高等级自智、智能体互联网等核心技术。鼓励基础电信企业用AI赋能传统业务，深化智慧个人助理、智慧管家、家庭看护、3D观影等新型应用。

items.title: 用好 Claude Design 的一些经验
items.description: 宝玉分享了5点心得：1. 加入设计系统（如 Adobe Spectrum 2）可避免 AI 味，设为默认后可专注布局与交互。2. 先搭建少量功能，再通过左侧聊天框逐步调整。3. 用 Markup 框选局部评论，Edit 可手动调整元素树。4. 注意上下文管理，新任务创建新会话。5. 通过 Tweaks 面板调整主题、布局、加载状态，也可添加导航快速切换界面。

items.title: 亚马逊的大规模扁平化数据中心网络
items.description: 亚马逊分享了在大规模数据中心中实现扁平化网络架构的工程实践与设计考量，重点论述了如何通过简化拓扑和路由策略来支撑超大规模集群的高带宽、低延迟通信。文章未披露具体模型或评测数据。

items.title: 2026年高考，跟着千问，选好志愿！
items.description: 千问发布国内首个全周期高考志愿填报Agent，由数百位资深高报师参与训练。该智能体提供AI志愿报告，为考生量身定制深度全面的填报方案；AI志愿日历帮助制定专属填报计划；高考专业知识库整合夸克高考8年积累，并引入志愿专家顾问，数据权威可信赖，全程陪伴考生完成志愿填报。

llmSummary.whyNow: Rapid star growth (2.8k in 7 days) and high heat score (420) indicate strong developer interest in cost optimization for LLM usage, especially as token costs remain a pain point.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers using LLM APIs frequently, engineering managers overseeing AI tooling budgets, and platform teams building internal developer tools.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Directly addresses the growing need to manage LLM API costs at scale, potentially reducing operational expenses for teams relying on AI-assisted development.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Offers a lightweight, drop-in solution to cut token consumption without modifying existing workflows, appealing to developers seeking immediate cost savings.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Token reduction claims (60-90%) are based on the project's own benchmarks; independent validation is lacking. Rapid star growth may reflect novelty rather than sustained adoption. No evidence of production deployment or enterprise use.

trendEntities.title: Claude Fable 5 和 Claude Mythos 5

items.title: Claude Fable 5 和 Claude Mythos 5
items.summary: Anthropic 今日推出 Claude Fable 5（通用安全版）和 Claude Mythos 5（受限安全版）。Fable 5 在软件工程、知识工作、视觉、科研等几乎所有测试基准上达到 SOTA，Stripe 称其将数月工程压缩至数天，FrontierCode 评分居前沿模型之首，可仅凭截图重建网页应用源码。Mythos 5 在药物设计中实现约 10 倍加速，其分子生物学假说盲测获科学家偏好的概率约 80%。两模型售价均为 $10/百万输入 tokens、$50/百万输出 tokens，较 Claude Mythos Preview 降价过半。Fable 5 在部分敏感主题上回退至 Claude Opus 4.8，安全触发率低于 5% 的会话。Mythos 5 通过 Project Glasswing 向网络安全防御者开放。

trendEntities.title: 百度百舸联合复旦提出LU-KV框架，被ICML 2026录用
normalizedKeys: title:百度百舸联合复旦提出lu kv框架 被icml 2026录用

items.title: 百度百舸联合复旦提出LU-KV框架，被ICML 2026录用
items.summary: 百度百舸团队与复旦大学合作提出Long-horizon Utility KV（LU-KV）框架，将头级KV Cache预算分配建模为面向长程边际效用的全局组合优化问题。LU-KV通过离线画像估计注意力头边际贡献曲线，结合凸包松弛与基于边际效用的贪心求解器，在较低开销下得到接近最优的预算配置，可适配SnapKV、KeyDiff等多类压缩方法。在LongBench和RULER基准上，80%压缩比下性能损失小，降低显存占用和推理延迟。相关论文被ICML 2026录用。

trendEntities.title: 华为云发布全球首个端到端具身AI平台CloudRobo
normalizedKeys: title:华为云发布全球首个端到端具身ai平台cloudrobo

items.title: 华为云发布全球首个端到端具身AI平台CloudRobo
items.summary: 华为云推出全球首个端到端具身AI开发平台CloudRobo，覆盖从数据、模型到部署、集成的全生命周期，基于PB级可信数据底座。在INSPIRE2026上，国家地方共建人形机器人创新中心、Yijiahe Technology、上海交通大学展示了其核心能力：数据与模型双评估系统、主动力控模型快速组装、机器人小时级上云、模型分钟级部署。

trendEntities.title: 谷歌 DeepMind 经济学家伊马斯：尚未发现 AI 造成岗位流失的证据，跟风裁员恐适得其反
normalizedKeys: title:谷歌 deepmind 经济学家伊马斯 尚未发现 ai 造成岗位流失的证据 跟风裁员恐适得其反

items.title: 谷歌 DeepMind 经济学家伊马斯：尚未发现 AI 造成岗位流失的证据，跟风裁员恐适得其反
items.summary: 谷歌 DeepMind AGI 经济学负责人亚历克斯·伊马斯表示，目前没有看到白领岗位因 AI 大规模消失的证据。他强调，若企业因"不裁员就等于 AI 转型慢"的叙事而跟风裁员，可能适得其反。伊马斯认为，AI 更多是接手部分任务、提升生产力，让员工专注机器无法完成的工作，岗位冲击尚未真正出现。

trendEntities.title: 摩尔线程开源 MusaCoder 代码大模型，9B/27B 参数基于国产 GPU 全链路训练
normalizedKeys: title:摩尔线程开源 musacoder 代码大模型 9b 27b 参数基于国产 gpu 全链路训练

items.title: 摩尔线程开源 MusaCoder 代码大模型，9B/27B 参数基于国产 GPU 全链路训练
items.summary: 摩尔线程发布并开源 MusaCoder 代码大模型，含 9B 和 27B 两个参数规模，是业内首个基于国产 GPU 算力底座完成全链路训练与验证的开源模型。后训练流程在基于 MTT S5000 的夸娥智算集群上完成，支持从 PyTorch 标准算子自动生成高性能 CUDA/MUSA 原生 Kernel 代码。在 KernelBench 评测中，MusaCoder-27B-RL 以 Overall Pass@8 93.2%、Avg.@8 88.60% 超越 Claude Opus、DeepSeek-V4 Pro、GLM-5.1、Kimi K2.6 等主流 SOTA 代码模型。
items.description: 摩尔线程发布并开源 MusaCoder 代码大模型，含 9B 和 27B 两个参数规模，是业内首个基于国产 GPU 算力底座完成全链路训练与验证的开源模型。后训练流程在基于 MTT S5000 的夸娥智算集群上完成，支持从 PyTorch 标准算子自动生成高性能 CUDA/MUSA 原生 Kernel 代码。在 KernelBench 评测中，MusaCoder-27B-RL 以 Overall Pass@8 93.2%、Avg.@8 88.60% 超越 Claude Opus、DeepSeek-V4 Pro、GLM-5.1、Kimi K2.6 等主流 SOTA 代码模型。

trendEntities.title: 工信部印发《"人工智能+信息通信"创新发展实施意见》
normalizedKeys: title:工信部印发 人工智能 信息通信 创新发展实施意见

items.title: 工信部印发《"人工智能+信息通信"创新发展实施意见》
items.summary: 工信部发文，要求加快建设400Gbps/800Gbps骨干传输网络，优化东中西部国家枢纽节点间通道；推进城域400Gbps及以上、全光交叉等高速光传输系统应用，构建城域毫秒级低时延入算能力。同时推动5G-A/6G、新一代光网络、"IPv6+"、工业互联网与AI融合发展，攻关空口智能化、网络高等级自智、智能体互联网等核心技术。鼓励基础电信企业用AI赋能传统业务，深化智慧个人助理、智慧管家、家庭看护、3D观影等新型应用。

trendEntities.title: 用好 Claude Design 的一些经验
normalizedKeys: title:用好 claude design 的一些经验

items.title: 用好 Claude Design 的一些经验
items.summary: 宝玉分享了5点心得：1. 加入设计系统（如 Adobe Spectrum 2）可避免 AI 味，设为默认后可专注布局与交互。2. 先搭建少量功能，再通过左侧聊天框逐步调整。3. 用 Markup 框选局部评论，Edit 可手动调整元素树。4. 注意上下文管理，新任务创建新会话。5. 通过 Tweaks 面板调整主题、布局、加载状态，也可添加导航快速切换界面。

items.title: 亚马逊的大规模扁平化数据中心网络
items.summary: 亚马逊分享了在大规模数据中心中实现扁平化网络架构的工程实践与设计考量，重点论述了如何通过简化拓扑和路由策略来支撑超大规模集群的高带宽、低延迟通信。文章未披露具体模型或评测数据。

trendEntities.title: 2026年高考，跟着千问，选好志愿！
normalizedKeys: title:2026年高考 跟着千问 选好志愿

items.title: 2026年高考，跟着千问，选好志愿！
items.summary: 千问发布国内首个全周期高考志愿填报Agent，由数百位资深高报师参与训练。该智能体提供AI志愿报告，为考生量身定制深度全面的填报方案；AI志愿日历帮助制定专属填报计划；高考专业知识库整合夸克高考8年积累，并引入志愿专家顾问，数据权威可信赖，全程陪伴考生完成志愿填报。

trendEntities.title: 火山方舟版权商业化平台上线，周星驰比高集团三大电影IP首批入驻
normalizedKeys: title:火山方舟版权商业化平台上线 周星驰比高集团三大电影ip首批入驻

items.title: 火山方舟版权商业化平台上线，周星驰比高集团三大电影IP首批入驻
items.summary: 火山引擎今日上线火山方舟版权商业化平台，推出行业首个覆盖"授权-保护-审核-分发-变现"全链路的版权合作机制。平台搭载视频生成模型Seedance 2.0及版权治理体系，已获周星驰旗下比高集团《喜剧之王》《食神》《长江七号》三部影片在AI视频创作场景下的版权使用权，并基于Seedance 2.0打造经典桥段AI创作模板。模板已在火山方舟体验中心、火山引擎Kickart上线，同步开放给LibTV、筷子科技丽帧引擎等工具合作伙伴。平台面向UGC和商业广告场景提供分润制、项目制等变现路径，未来还将提供版权管理后台，实现授权可见、使用可查、收入透明。

trendEntities.title: Magnetar用数百AI智能体替代分析师
normalizedKeys: title:magnetar用数百ai智能体替代分析师

items.title: Magnetar用数百AI智能体替代分析师
items.summary: Bloomberg：Magnetar Capital，这家 180 亿美元的对冲基金公司，将在其最新产品中避免使用人类分析师，转而依靠数百个 AI 智能体进行股票研究。 这家 180 亿美元的对冲基金公司希望 AI 搜索投资想法、研究公司、推荐头寸并预测趋势，而人类仍负责批准交易。

trendEntities.title: 欧盟发布临时措施，要求 Meta 向第三方 AI 助手免费开放 WhatsApp
normalizedKeys: title:欧盟发布临时措施 要求 meta 向第三方 ai 助手免费开放 whatsapp

items.title: 欧盟发布临时措施，要求 Meta 向第三方 AI 助手免费开放 WhatsApp
items.summary: 欧盟委员会6月9日宣布临时措施，责令Meta在反垄断调查结束前免费向第三方AI助手开放WhatsApp访问权限。Meta于2025年10月15日禁止第三方AI助手调用WhatsApp for Business API，意图让自家Meta AI独占市场；今年3月4日虽改为付费使用，但欧盟委员会认为这实质上延续了禁令，可能严重损害通用AI助手市场竞争，尤其危及小企业和新进入者。

normalizedKeys: title:text to lottie agent skill 本地预览 harness 让 agent 生成 lottie 动画并实时验收

items.title: Text-To-Lottie：Agent Skill + 本地预览 Harness，让 Agent 生成 Lottie 动画并实时验收
items.summary: 开源项目 Text-To-Lottie 提供一套 Agent Skill 和本地预览工具，让 Codex/Claude Code/Cursor 等 Agent 生成标准 Bodymovin JSON（public/lottie.json），通过 Skottie 渲染引擎在浏览器中实时验收。安装命令：`npx skills add diffusionstudio/lottie`。技术特点：输出标准 Lottie JSON，使用 Skottie 渲染（非 lottie-web）；基于 Vite 热重载实现自动刷新闭环；支持通过 Skottie Slots + controls.json 调整颜色、尺寸；Agent 可用 URL 参数 `？frame=60&paused=1` 精确定位帧截图。Prompt 指南包括：给具体素材、使用动效术语、pan/zoom/hold 模拟镜头、显式声明 Slots、锁定时间规格。适合单场景短时长 Motion Graphics、SVG→Lottie、数据可视化、透明背景矢量动效；不适合多镜头剪辑、复杂角色绑定、粒子、3D 或需 lottie-web 全特性的场景。

trendEntities.title: IBM CEO：AI不一定导致员工减少
normalizedKeys: title:ibm ceo ai不一定导致员工减少

items.title: IBM CEO：AI不一定导致员工减少
items.summary: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna表示AI不会必然导致员工数量减少。他透露IBM已在量子计算（一种更快形式的AI）上投资100亿美元，并指出联邦政府承诺投入10亿美元在纽约Albany建设芯片制造设施，体现了公私部门间的紧密合作。

trendEntities.title: 在 AgentsView 中为 Claude Fable 5 设置自定义价格
normalizedKeys: title:在 agentsview 中为 claude fable 5 设置自定义价格

items.title: 在 AgentsView 中为 Claude Fable 5 设置自定义价格
items.summary: Wes McKinney 开发的 AgentsView 是一个用于追踪本地编码智能体 token 使用情况的工具。由于近日发布的 Claude Fable 5 尚未被收录进 AgentsView 的定价数据库，作者利用 Fable 逆向工程，找到了为该模型设置自定义价格的方法，并展示了 Fable 5 当天在不同本地项目中的使用量树状图。

items.title: Super Micro 计划通过股权融资 70 亿美元用于 AI 服务器组件采购
items.summary: Super Micro Computer Inc. 计划通过一揽子股权融资筹集 70 亿美元，用于购买客户订单所需的 AI 服务器组件。这笔资金将支持公司扩大产能，以满足不断增长的人工智能基础设施需求。

trendEntities.title: Claude Managed Agents 新增定时运行和环境变量存储功能
normalizedKeys: title:claude managed agents 新增定时运行和环境变量存储功能

items.title: Claude Managed Agents 新增定时运行和环境变量存储功能
items.summary: Claude Managed Agents 今日在 Claude Platform 公开测试两项新功能：代理可按 cron 计划自动执行周期性任务（如夜间数据同步、周度合规扫描、每日摘要），无需用户自建调度器，支持暂停、恢复、归档或按需触发；vaults 新增环境变量支持，允许代理通过 CLI 进行认证请求，真实密钥仅附加在网络边界，代理无法读取。已集成的 CLI 包括 Browserbase、KERNEL、Notion、Ramp 和 Sentry。Rakuten、Actively AI、Ando、Milana 等团队正在使用这些功能实现自动化数据报表、跨账户搜索、招聘提醒等场景。

trendEntities.title: Hugging Face 博客发布语音智能体代码切换基准测试
normalizedKeys: title:hugging face 博客发布语音智能体代码切换基准测试

items.title: Hugging Face 博客发布语音智能体代码切换基准测试
items.summary: Hugging Face 博客发布针对语音智能体处理代码切换语音的基准测试。数据集覆盖西班牙语-英语、法语-英语、加拿大法语-英语和德语-英语四对语言，基于人力资源与IT服务管理场景构建。采用词错误率、语义词错误率和答案错误率三项指标评估七种ASR系统，包括AssemblyAI Universal 3-Pro、Deepgram Nova 3 Multilang、ElevenLabs Scribe V2、Gemini 3 Flash、Mistral AI Voxtral Small 24B-2507、Nvidia Parakeet TDT 0.6b V3和OpenAI Whisper Large V3 Turbo。主要发现：代码切换的转录成本因语言对和模型而异；ElevenLabs Scribe V2、Gemini 3 Flash和AssemblyAI Universal 3-Pro在所有指标上表现最佳。数据集和测试框架通过AU-Harness开源发布。

trendEntities.title: Claude Code 团队 Thariq 分享提升 Claude Code 效率的十条建议
normalizedKeys: title:claude code 团队 thariq 分享提升 claude code 效率的十条建议

items.title: Claude Code 团队 Thariq 分享提升 Claude Code 效率的十条建议
items.summary: Thariq（Claude Code 团队）提出十条建议，核心转变是：从检查 Claude 是否做对工作，转向检查它是否在做正确的工作。具体包括：提前提供完整上下文，将其视为思考伙伴；用小规格文档让 Claude 访谈实现细节；探索多方向并生成 HTML 原型；提供丰富上下文（如功能可能一个月后删除）而非硬约束；设定明确目标与验证方法；使用 /goal 命令；利用 Workflows 并行任务、自我验证并生成对比报告；同时设置目标和 workflow；更勇敢地将此前认为 LLM 无法完成的任务交给 Claude Fable 5，因其可运行数小时、自检并产出高质量代码。Thariq 本人用 Claude Fable 5 剪辑了整段视频证明其能力。

trendEntities.title: NotebookLM 笔记本功能在 Gemini App 欧洲全面上线
normalizedKeys: title:notebooklm 笔记本功能在 gemini app 欧洲全面上线

items.title: NotebookLM 笔记本功能在 Gemini App 欧洲全面上线
items.summary: NotbookLM 宣布其笔记本功能已在欧洲的 Gemini App 中 100% 上线。此前用户只能上传笔记本作为 Gemini 的来源，现在可直接在 Gemini App 内访问所有个人未共享的笔记本，并将与 Gemini 的对话作为新笔记本或已有笔记本的来源。该功能先面向 Google AI Ultra、Pro 和 Plus 订阅者的网页端，未来几周将扩展到移动端、更多欧洲国家及免费用户。

trendEntities.title: OpenRouter 推出 Advisor 工具：让低成本模型可随时调用强模型增强生成
normalizedKeys: title:openrouter 推出 advisor 工具 让低成本模型可随时调用强模型增强生成

items.title: OpenRouter 推出 Advisor 工具：让低成本模型可随时调用强模型增强生成
items.summary: OpenRouter 发布 advisor 服务器工具，允许一个快速、便宜的模型在生成过程中咨询一个更强大的模型。具体而言，可用 GPT-4o Mini 处理日常例行工作，在关键时刻调用 Claude Fable 解决真正重要的问题，从而实现成本和质量的动态平衡。

trendEntities.title: Claude Fable 发布：Anthropic 带来的另一种推理体验
normalizedKeys: title:claude fable 发布 anthropic 带来的另一种推理体验

items.title: Claude Fable 发布：Anthropic 带来的另一种推理体验
items.summary: Anthropic 发布 Claude Fable，这是一款提供截然不同推理体验的 AI 模型。它擅长规划与生成复杂代码库，在需要精确构建代码结构或理解程序员深层需求的场景中，其表现相比 Claude Sonnet 有了大幅提升。用户描述与它协作更像与一位直觉敏锐的资深工程师合作，其对代码意图的捕捉和方案生成能力令人惊叹，但并非通用型 AI。

items.title: Apollo 与 Blackstone 联手 350 亿美元 AI 融资交易
items.summary: Apollo 和 Blackstone 合作开展 350 亿美元 AI 融资交易，可能重塑人工智能基础设施的融资方式。华尔街正为昂贵的 AI 芯片创建新的融资模型，Anthropic 和 Broadcom 参与其中。这笔交易可能标志着一个全新 AI 投资类别的开端。

trendEntities.title: When to Align, When to Predict: A Phase Diagram for Multimodal Learning
normalizedKeys: title:when to align when to predict a phase diagram for multimodal learning

items.title: When to Align, When to Predict: A Phase Diagram for Multimodal Learning
items.description: Cross-modal alignment (CA) and cross-modal prediction (CP) are the dominant paradigms for multimodal representation learning, yet there is no systematic understanding of when each succeeds, when each fails, and when cross-modal training helps at all -- a gap that leaves practitioners, especially in scientific domains like biomedicine or astrophysics, with heterogeneous instruments and multiple levels of organization and measurement, unable to diagnose why standard methods underperform the best s
items.summary: Cross-modal alignment (CA) and cross-modal prediction (CP) are the dominant paradigms for multimodal representation learning, yet there is no systematic understanding of when each succeeds, when each fails, and when cross-modal training helps at all -- a gap that leaves practitioners, especially in scientific domains like biomedicine or astrophysics, with heterogeneous instruments and multiple levels of organization and measurement, unable to diagnose why standard methods underperform the best single modality. We develop a unified linear framework that addresses both questions. Under a spiked signal-plus-noise model with structured cross-modal nuisance correlation, we derive separation ratios for both objectives that expose complementary failure modes: alignment whitens each modality and fails when nuisance is strongly correlated across views; prediction encodes whatever is cross-predictable through a one-sided whitening, with recovery governed by source-modality quality. The resulting phase diagram partitions multimodal problems into four regimes: Both, CA only, CP only, and Neither. We present a data-driven procedure to locate real-world datasets in this diagram using a small labeled subsample, identifying the preferred objective and prediction direction before any cross-modal training. Experiments on synthetic data, stereo-vision benchmarks, image-caption pairs, and real astrophysical data validate the predictions in the nonlinear regime, including the Neither regime where cross-modal training is actively harmful. Our framework lets practitioners diagnose their multimodal problem and choose the right objective before committing to training. Code to reproduce the results is available at https://github.com/IlayMalinyak/mm_align_vs_pred.
items.author: Ilay Kamai, Hugues Van Assel, Aviv Regev, Hagai B. Perets, Randall Balestriero
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: alignment, benchmark

trendEntities.title: A Unifying Lens on Supervised Fine-Tuning Through Target Distribution Design
normalizedKeys: title:a unifying lens on supervised fine tuning through target distribution design

items.title: A Unifying Lens on Supervised Fine-Tuning Through Target Distribution Design
items.description: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) typically maximizes the likelihood of every token in a demonstrated trajectory. However, an observed token can be non-unique, noisy, or misaligned with the model prior. Strictly fitting toward this one-hot target may be suboptimal, especially when the pretrained model encodes a rich knowledge prior. items.summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) typically maximizes the likelihood of every token in a demonstrated trajectory. However, an observed token can be non-unique, noisy, or misaligned with the model prior. Strictly fitting toward this one-hot target may be suboptimal, especially when the pretrained model encodes a rich knowledge prior. In this work, we reinterpret SFT as target distribution design: instead of studying only the loss objective, we analyze the token-level target that the loss drives the model to match. We introduce the Q-target framework, which decomposes SFT supervision into two explicit choices: (1) how strongly to rely on the observed token, and (2) how to allocate the remaining probability mass over alternatives. This perspective unifies many existing SFT variants as implicit choices of the target distribution Q. Building on this view, we propose Target-SFT which constructs the training objective directly from the desired target distribution. This method consistently outperforms across the ten reasoning dataset-model settings evaluated, showing the effectiveness of this target-based approach. Overall, our formulation reveals a more fundamental design principle for SFT training and opens a broader search space for SFT objectives.
items.author: Tong Xie, Yuanhao Ban, Yunqi Hong, Sohyun An, Yihang Chen, Cho-Jui Hsieh
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: fine-tuning

items.title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents
items.description: In this paper, we propose EEVEE, the first multi-dataset test-time prompt learning framework for LLM agents, enabling test-time prompt learning under real-world task streams. Existing methods are largely designed for single-dataset settings, while real-world applications require models to handle heterogeneous input streams drawn from multiple datasets, domains, and task distributions, limiting their practical applicability. To mitigate cross-dataset interference, EEVEE introduces a router that p
items.summary: In this paper, we propose EEVEE, the first multi-dataset test-time prompt learning framework for LLM agents, enabling test-time prompt learning under real-world task streams. Existing methods are largely designed for single-dataset settings, while real-world applications require models to handle heterogeneous input streams drawn from multiple datasets, domains, and task distributions, limiting their practical applicability. To mitigate cross-dataset interference, EEVEE introduces a router that partitions incoming inputs into task clusters and assigns them to suitable prompt configurations. This design is optimized via a router-prompt co-evolution strategy, which employs interleaved router and prompt learning phases to address their mutual dependency. Experiments across multiple datasets demonstrate that the framework improves robustness under heterogeneous data streams while maintaining single-benchmark learning capability and efficiency. Specifically, EEVEE improves average multi-benchmark scores by 10.38 and 24.32 points over Qwen3-4B-Instruct and DeepSeek-V3.2, surpassing SOTA methods GEPA and ACE by up to 37.2% and 48.2%.
items.author: Weixian Xu, Shilong Liu, Mengdi Wang
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, rag, benchmark

trendEntities.title: Data Journalist Agent: Transforming Data into Verifiable Multimodal Stories
normalizedKeys: title:data journalist agent transforming data into verifiable multimodal stories

items.title: Data Journalist Agent: Transforming Data into Verifiable Multimodal Stories
items.description: Data tells stories that shape society; the data journalist's job is to turn raw information into stories non-experts can trust. A high-quality news feature takes a newsroom team weeks: hunting for context, running statistics, choosing an angle, and designing visuals. items.summary: Data tells stories that shape society; the data journalist's job is to turn raw information into stories non-experts can trust. A high-quality news feature takes a newsroom team weeks: hunting for context, running statistics, choosing an angle, and designing visuals. Recent agents handle individual steps well: data-science agents close the analysis loop, while design agents synthesize beautiful websites. But can an agent serve as a data journalist end to end? We introduce Data Journalist Agent (Data2Story), a multi-agent framework that orchestrates specialized roles into a single virtual newsroom. Data2Story contributes two innovations. (i) Claims are evidence-grounded: an Inspector links every number, angle, and asset back to data, code, or an external reference. (ii) Articles are multimodally generative: rather than defaulting to plain text and static charts, Data2Story reasons about what readers will want to see, then deploys multimodal tools, such as interactive maps for geography and audio for music. We evaluate Data2Story on 18 articles, each paired with the originally published expert piece, along four axes: (a) human-agent angle coverage; (b) rubric evaluation with 53 participants across five dimensions; (c) computer-use agents as judges, a cost-saving proxy for how readers navigate interactive articles; and (d) verifiability, where a coding verifier re-executes statements against the data and checks claims against references. Data2Story produces competitive, evidence-traceable multimedia stories, with particular strength in transparency and auditability. Human articles retain an edge in editorial angle, creative design, and presentation. We position Data2Story as a collaborator for journalists, enabling more evidence-based, transparent, and verifiable reporting. Code and demos are available at https://data2story.github.io.
items.author: Kevin Qinghong Lin, Batu EI, Yuhong Shi, Pan Lu, Philip Torr, James Zou
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, multi-agent, rag, evaluation

sourceItems.summary: Data tells stories that shape society; the data journalist's job is to turn raw information into stories non-experts can trust. A high-quality news feature takes a newsroom team weeks: hunting for context, running statistics, choosing an angle, and designing visuals. Recent agents handle individual steps well: data-science agents close the analysis loop, while design agents synthesize beautiful websites. But can an agent serve as a data journalist end to end? We introduce Data Journalist Agent (Data2Story), a multi-agent framework that orchestrates specialized roles into a single virtual newsroom. Data2Story contributes two innovations. (i) Claims are evidence-grounded: an Inspector links every number, angle, and asset back to data, code, or an external reference. (ii) Articles are multimodally generative: rather than defaulting to plain text and static charts, Data2Story reasons about what readers will want to see, then deploys multimodal tools, such as interactive maps for geography and audio for music. We evaluate Data2Story on 18 articles, each paired with the originally published expert piece, along four axes: (a) human-agent angle coverage; (b) rubric evaluation with 53 participants across five dimensions; (c) computer-use agents as judges, a cost-saving proxy for how readers navigate interactive articles; and (d) verifiability, where a coding verifier re-executes statements against the data and checks claims against references. Data2Story produces competitive, evidence-traceable multimedia stories, with particular strength in transparency and auditability. Human articles retain an edge in editorial angle, creative design, and presentation. We position Data2Story as a collaborator for journalists, enabling more evidence-based, transparent, and verifiable reporting. Code and demos are available at https://data2story.github.io.
sourceItems.author: Kevin Qinghong Lin, Batu EI, Yuhong Shi, Pan Lu, Philip Torr, James Zou

trendEntities.title: The Role of Feedback Alignment in Self-Distillation
normalizedKeys: title:the role of feedback alignment in self distillation

items.title: The Role of Feedback Alignment in Self-Distillation
items.description: Conditioning a language model on additional context, such as feedback on a previous attempt, typically improves its response. Self-distillation trains the model to retain this improvement when the context is not present. The method works by matching the model's output distribution under two settings: a student that sees only the question, and a self-teacher that also sees the context. What the model learns therefore depends on what context the self-teacher receives, yet the design of this contex
items.summary: Conditioning a language model on additional context, such as feedback on a previous attempt, typically improves its response. Self-distillation trains the model to retain this improvement when the context is not present. The method works by matching the model's output distribution under two settings: a student that sees only the question, and a self-teacher that also sees the context. What the model learns therefore depends on what context the self-teacher receives, yet the design of this context remains largely unexplored. We study context design for self-distillation by training a solver on feedback from a frozen critic. We compare three conditions: (i) a binary reward (GRPO), (ii) the reference solution, and (iii) a step-by-step critique aligned to the solver's reasoning trace. Step-aligned critique yields the largest gains, outperforming GRPO by 16.11 points and reference-solution-conditioned self-distillation by 5.27 points (Avg@12). Per-token advantage analysis reveals why: step-aligned feedback targets only the tokens where reasoning fails, leaving correct behavior intact. Conditioning on the reference solution, by contrast, pressures the model to change its behavior at every token (even correct steps) because an alternative derivation inevitably differs in phrasing and approach. This suggests that structural alignment between feedback and the solver's reasoning is a key driver of self-distillation effectiveness.
items.author: Semih Kara, Oğuzhan Ersoy

trendEntities.title: Predicting Future Behaviors in Reasoning Models Enables Better Steering
normalizedKeys: title:predicting future behaviors in reasoning models enables better steering

items.title: Predicting Future Behaviors in Reasoning Models Enables Better Steering
items.description: Deployed large reasoning models (LRMs) often behave unexpectedly. Test-time steering controls LRM outputs by intervening on their hidden representations, but it can degrade output quality. We argue that prior steering work implicitly relies on internal features that detect behavior in already generated text. We show that these detection features are poor predictors of future behavioral outcomes, and thus not the natural intervention target. Instead, we train activation probes to predict future b
items.summary: Deployed large reasoning models (LRMs) often behave unexpectedly. Test-time steering controls LRM outputs by intervening on their hidden representations, but it can degrade output quality. We argue that prior steering work implicitly relies on internal features that detect behavior in already generated text. We show that these detection features are poor predictors of future behavioral outcomes, and thus not the natural intervention target. Instead, we train activation probes to predict future behavior likelihoods from intermediate reasoning steps. These probes predict the most likely behavior with 64%-91% accuracy, revealing a separate type of internal prediction features. Building on these prediction features, we introduce a text-level steering method, Future Probe Controlled Generation. FPCG samples multiple candidate sentences and chooses the best one according to a probe predicting the future behavior likelihood. This enables steering with almost no output quality degradation. FPCG also enables steering in several evaluations where activation steering fails. These results show that distinguishing detection and prediction features enables a more nuanced approach to controlling LRM behaviors.
items.author: Evgenii Kortukov, Piotr Komorowski, Florian Klein, Paula Engl, Gabriele Sarti, Seong Joon Oh, Sebastian Lapuschkin, Wojciech Samek

trendEntities.title: Multi-Faceted Interactivity Alignment in Full-Duplex Speech Models
normalizedKeys: title:multi faceted interactivity alignment in full duplex speech models

items.title: Multi-Faceted Interactivity Alignment in Full-Duplex Speech Models
items.description: Full-duplex spoken dialogue models can listen and speak simultaneously, making them a promising architecture for natural conversation. However, current models are trained solely with supervised learning through token-level likelihood maximization, which does not directly optimize interaction-level behaviors, causing interactivity issues such as excessive silence and ill-timed turn-taking. Recent work has applied reinforcement learning (RL) to improve interactivity, but existing methods address o
items.summary: Full-duplex spoken dialogue models can listen and speak simultaneously, making them a promising architecture for natural conversation. However, current models are trained solely with supervised learning through token-level likelihood maximization, which does not directly optimize interaction-level behaviors, causing interactivity issues such as excessive silence and ill-timed turn-taking. Recent work has applied reinforcement learning (RL) to improve interactivity, but existing methods address only a limited set of interactive behaviors in their rewards. In this work, we propose a post-training alignment method that comprehensively improves the interactivity of full-duplex spoken dialogue models through RL. We address the four canonical axes of interactivity: pause handling, turn-taking, backchanneling, and user interruption. For each axis, we extract short audio segments from human conversation corpora and optimize the model with axis-specific reward functions. An extra LLM-based reward for response quality prevents semantic degradation. We apply our method to two open-source models, Moshi and PersonaPlex, demonstrating consistent improvements in interactivity on both offline evaluation with pre-recorded audio and real-time multi-turn dialogue evaluation.
items.author: Atsumoto Ohashi, Neil Zeghidour, Alexandre Défossez, Eugene Kharitonov
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, alignment, evaluation

trendEntities.title: Flaws in the LLM Automation Narrative
normalizedKeys: title:flaws in the llm automation narrative

items.title: Flaws in the LLM Automation Narrative
items.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly described as performing at the level of human experts on knowledge economy tasks. These claims are primarily based on how LLMs perform on benchmarking tasks that measure average performance across standardized datasets. Primary limitations of many benchmarking tasks are that they often measure performance based on content directly included in LLM training data, and they frequently do not assess the reliability of LLM performance or the magnitude of L
items.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly described as performing at the level of human experts on knowledge economy tasks. These claims are primarily based on how LLMs perform on benchmarking tasks that measure average performance across standardized datasets. Primary limitations of many benchmarking tasks are that they often measure performance based on content directly included in LLM training data, and they frequently do not assess the reliability of LLM performance or the magnitude of LLM errors. However, in high stakes contexts, these qualities are critically important. Through a novel LLM benchmarking task that requires writing computer code to complete a data analysis task, we compare the performance of a frontier LLM against submissions from human experts and explicitly measure the variance of responses and the magnitude of errors. Our study reveals that the human experts perform better on average on a range of metrics and demonstrate less variability in performance. Our results provide evidence that LLMs do not consistently perform at the level of human experts and demonstrate the importance of measuring variance and assessing error magnitude in LLM benchmark evaluations.
items.author: George Perrett, Javae Elliott, Jennifer Hill, Marc Scott

trendEntities.title: ReasonAlloc: Hierarchical Decoding-Time KV Cache Budget Allocation for Reasoning Models
normalizedKeys: title:reasonalloc hierarchical decoding time kv cache budget allocation for reasoning models

items.title: ReasonAlloc: Hierarchical Decoding-Time KV Cache Budget Allocation for Reasoning Models
items.description: Long chain-of-thought (CoT) trajectories in large language model (LLM) reasoning cause severe inference bottlenecks due to rapid key-value (KV) cache growth. Current decoding-time compression methods mitigate this issue via token eviction, but typically assume a uniform budget distribution across all layers and heads. In contrast, existing non-uniform budget allocation methods are predominantly designed for the static prompt prefill phase, and they do not capture the stepwise context demands of
items.summary: Long chain-of-thought (CoT) trajectories in large language model (LLM) reasoning cause severe inference bottlenecks due to rapid key-value (KV) cache growth. Current decoding-time compression methods mitigate this issue via token eviction, but typically assume a uniform budget distribution across all layers and heads. In contrast, existing non-uniform budget allocation methods are predominantly designed for the static prompt prefill phase, and they do not capture the stepwise context demands of autoregressive reasoning. To bridge this gap, we propose ReasonAlloc, a training-free framework that recasts decoding-time KV compression as a hierarchical budget allocation problem. ReasonAlloc operates at two complementary levels: an offline layer-wise preallocation strategy captures an architecture-driven demand pattern which we call ``\textit{Reasoning Wave}'', while an online head-wise strategy reallocates resources during decoding to information-rich heads based on real-time utility. Evaluations on mathematical reasoning benchmarks (MATH-500, AIME~2024) using DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-14B, and AceReason-14B show that ReasonAlloc outperforms uniform-budget R-KV, SnapKV, and Pyramid-RKV (a baseline enforcing a static, monotonically decreasing layer budget), with the largest gains at small budgets (128-512 tokens). ReasonAlloc is plug-and-play with existing token-eviction policies and introduces negligible inference-time overhead.
items.author: Wenhao Liu, Hao Shi, Yunhe Li, Weizhi Fei, Xiangyuan Wang, Mengzhe Ruan, Hanxu Hou, Peisong Wang, Linqi Song, Shuang Qiu

trendEntities.title: Itô maps for any-step SDEs
normalizedKeys: title:itô maps for any step sdes

items.title: Itô maps for any-step SDEs
items.description: Recent one-step generative models accelerate sampling by learning deterministic flow maps of the underlying dynamics. These methods rely on learning from ordinary differential equations, leaving open how to define an exact distillation procedure for stochastic dynamics. We introduce the Itô map, an any-step stochastic flow map that takes an intermediate state and Brownian path and predicts future states in a single pass. The Itô map formulation yields novel estimators for inference-time control
items.summary: Recent one-step generative models accelerate sampling by learning deterministic flow maps of the underlying dynamics. These methods rely on learning from ordinary differential equations, leaving open how to define an exact distillation procedure for stochastic dynamics. We introduce the Itô map, an any-step stochastic flow map that takes an intermediate state and Brownian path and predicts future states in a single pass. The Itô map formulation yields novel estimators for inference-time control by providing cheap, differentiable access to posterior samples. Empirically, Itô maps produce diverse, conditionally valid endpoint samples from fixed intermediate states and support strong steering performance on synthetic and image-generation benchmarks. These results establish any-step SDE integration as a useful primitive for posterior sampling and stochastic control.
items.author: Zhengkai Pan, Peter Potaptchik, Wenxi Yao, Michael S. Albergo, Jakiw Pidstrigach

trendEntities.title: ABC-Bench: An Agentic Bio-Capabilities Benchmark for Biosecurity
normalizedKeys: title:abc bench an agentic bio capabilities benchmark for biosecurity

items.title: ABC-Bench: An Agentic Bio-Capabilities Benchmark for Biosecurity
items.description: Large language models (LLMs) are rapidly acquiring capabilities relevant to biological research, from literature synthesis to interpretation of experimental data. Increasingly, LLM agents can also perform in silico biology tasks that previously required experienced human biologists. These emerging AI capabilities offer new opportunities for scientific discovery and biomedical advances, but they also shift the landscape of biosecurity risks. items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are rapidly acquiring capabilities relevant to biological research, from literature synthesis to interpretation of experimental data. Increasingly, LLM agents can also perform in silico biology tasks that previously required experienced human biologists. These emerging AI capabilities offer new opportunities for scientific discovery and biomedical advances, but they also shift the landscape of biosecurity risks. To address this, we introduce the Agentic Bio-Capabilities Benchmark (ABC-Bench), a suite of tasks to measure agentic biosecurity-relevant capabilities. ABC-Bench evaluates LLM agents on both benign and dual-use biology tasks: writing code to operate liquid handling robots, designing DNA fragments for in vitro assembly, and evading DNA synthesis screening. These tasks require a combination of biology and software expertise. All tested LLM agents outperformed the median expert human baseliner on all three tasks. Agents performed highly on tasks drawing on published knowledge and well-documented protocols, and more weakly on a task requiring novel bioinformatics reasoning. In three wet-lab validation experiments, we found that OpenAI's o4-mini-high produced scripts that, when run on an OpenTrons liquid handling robot, successfully assembled DNA with expected sequences.
items.author: Andrew Bo Liu, Samira Nedungadi, Bryce Cai, Alex Kleinman, Harmon Bhasin, Seth Donoughe
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, agent, rag, benchmark
